panels.title: Token Distribution by Prompt Type
panels.description: Shows what percentage of tokens go to tool interactions vs human conversation. If tool_use/tool_result dominate, most cost comes from Claude reading files and running commands, not from your prompts.

targets.rawSql: SELECT m.prompt_type, SUM(COALESCE(t.input_tokens, 0) + COALESCE(t.output_tokens, 0) + COALESCE(t.cache_read_tokens, 0) + COALESCE(t.cache_creation_tokens, 0)) AS total_tokens FROM messages m LEFT JOIN token_usage t ON t.message_uuid = m.message_uuid WHERE m.timestamp BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo() GROUP BY m.prompt_type ORDER BY total_tokens DESC

panels.title: Average Tokens per Message by Prompt Type
panels.description: A single tool_result (file read) might cost 10x more than a human_input. Identifies which interaction types are most expensive per message, helping you understand where optimization efforts should focus.

targets.rawSql: SELECT m.prompt_type, AVG(COALESCE(t.input_tokens, 0) + COALESCE(t.output_tokens, 0) + COALESCE(t.cache_read_tokens, 0) + COALESCE(t.cache_creation_tokens, 0)) AS avg_tokens, COUNT(*) AS message_count FROM messages m LEFT JOIN token_usage t ON t.message_uuid = m.message_uuid WHERE m.timestamp BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo() GROUP BY m.prompt_type ORDER BY avg_tokens DESC

panels.title: Prompt Type Mix Over Time
panels.description: Shows how your usage pattern evolves. Are you relying more on tool-heavy workflows over time? A growing share of tool_use/tool_result indicates increasingly autonomous Claude sessions. Normalized to 100% to reveal proportional shifts regardless of volume changes.

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('day', m.timestamp) AS time, m.prompt_type, SUM(COALESCE(t.input_tokens, 0) + COALESCE(t.output_tokens, 0)) AS tokens FROM messages m LEFT JOIN token_usage t ON t.message_uuid = m.message_uuid WHERE m.timestamp BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo() GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Message Count by Prompt Type Over Time
panels.description: Volume of each message type over time. Combined with average tokens per message, reveals whether cost comes from many cheap messages or few expensive ones. A spike in tool_result count means Claude is doing more file reads or command executions.

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('day', m.timestamp) AS time, m.prompt_type, COUNT(*) AS messages FROM messages m WHERE m.timestamp BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo() GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Human vs Automated Token Ratio
panels.description: Ratio of human_input tokens to total tokens. Shows how much of the conversation is 'you talking' vs 'Claude working autonomously'. A low ratio means Claude is doing a lot of tool work per human prompt — high leverage. A declining trend suggests you're becoming more efficient at prompting.

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('day', m.timestamp) AS time, CASE WHEN SUM(COALESCE(t.input_tokens, 0) + COALESCE(t.output_tokens, 0)) = 0 THEN 0 ELSE 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN m.prompt_type = 'human_input' THEN COALESCE(t.input_tokens, 0) + COALESCE(t.output_tokens, 0) ELSE 0 END) / SUM(COALESCE(t.input_tokens, 0) + COALESCE(t.output_tokens, 0)) END AS human_pct FROM messages m LEFT JOIN token_usage t ON t.message_uuid = m.message_uuid WHERE m.timestamp BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo() GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Most Token-Heavy Tool Interactions
panels.description: Top tool_result and tool_use messages by token count. Reveals which specific tool calls are most expensive — e.g., reading large files, long bash outputs, or costly Write operations. Use this to identify optimization targets like reducing file reads or splitting large operations.

targets.rawSql: SELECT m.prompt_type, m.tool_name, LEFT(m.prompt_text, 100) AS prompt_preview, t.input_tokens, t.output_tokens, t.cache_read_tokens, t.input_tokens + t.output_tokens + t.cache_read_tokens + t.cache_creation_tokens AS total_tokens, s.project, m.timestamp FROM messages m JOIN token_usage t ON t.message_uuid = m.message_uuid JOIN sessions s ON s.session_id = m.session_id WHERE m.timestamp BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo() AND m.prompt_type IN ('tool_use', 'tool_result') ORDER BY total_tokens DESC LIMIT 50
